page: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8559C processor. This x86_64 architecture platform features 48 virtual CPUs (24 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 2.4 GHz, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. It includes 384 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 11,250 GB of NVMe SSD storage. This high-density local storage is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 28 Gbps. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory and direct-attached NVMe storage makes the instance highly capable of handling data-intensive workloads without external storage bottlenecks.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across compute, memory, and database operations. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2319.0 and a multi-core score of 20704.0, placing it in the top tier of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block speeds of 4099.27 GB/sec and large block write speeds of 123.85 GB/sec. Database performance is highlighted by a Passmark database operations score of 26527.8 and Redis SET operations reaching 72.52 million operations per second. Additionally, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support contributes to strong LLM inference speeds, processing 368.87 tokens per second on small models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.12xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe storage and DDR5 memory relative to its compute resources, reducing the need for expensive external block storage attachments. This resource profile is optimal for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL databases, real-time analytics, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores, it features 384 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers high-speed data access, supported by a 28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place the server in the top tier for single-core and multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 20,704.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 72.52 million operations per second. The hardware configuration, featuring Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, provides strong performance for database operations, memory-cached applications, and local data processing. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, real-time analytics, and high-throughput storage workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and analytics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,250 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
11,250 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
384 GB of fast DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier database performance with 72.52 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for large-scale databases, real-time analytics, and data-intensive applications
